content:
Operational Spec: Multi-Dimensional Telemetry & Alerting

Objective: convert noisy, siloed signals into actionable operational intelligence with MTTR-focused anomaly detection and SLO-aligned alerting.

1. Core Foundations

Noise Reduction

Reduce alert fatigue via suppression, deduplication, and cardinality-aware alert design. Only high-severity, customer-impacting events trigger immediate notifications.

SLO Alignment

Map technical telemetry to Service Level Objectives so on-call actions directly reflect availability and response-time requirements.

2. Telemetry Schema (Prometheus Scraping Strategy)

Implemented a Prometheus scraping strategy across hybrid-cloud planes using exporters (Node, SNMP, Process) to capture multi-dimensional metrics. Cardinality is managed with label-based cardinality constraints to prevent TSDB performance degradation.

LABEL-BASED CARDINALITY MANAGEMENT

Rules:
  - cap high-cardinality labels
  - normalize label values
  - aggregate early
  - enforce recording rules for derived metrics

3. Pipeline Logic (Logstash Enrichment Layer)

Integrated ELK Stack with Logstash filters that transform raw syslogs into structured JSON events. Grok patterns extract key fields, then events are enriched with security context to support forensic queries.

Grok Transformation + JSON Normalization

Drop vanity logs and retain high-value events so dashboards and alerts stay actionable under load.

Security Context Enrichment

Enrich with identity/network metadata so SOC workflows correlate activity with telemetry and SLO impact.

4. Threshold Engineering (Delta Alerting)

Transitioned from static thresholds to Rate-of-Change (Delta) Alerting using PromQL to reduce false-positive cardinality during peak load.

DELTA ALERT EXAMPLE (PromQL)

alert: DiskIOAnomaly
expr: (rate(node_disk_read_bytes_total[5m])
        - rate(node_disk_read_bytes_total[1h])[1m])
      / rate(node_disk_read_bytes_total[1h])[1m] > 0.25
for: 2m

5. SLO/SLI Alignment

Technical metrics are mapped to high-level SLO/SLIs so alerting reflects customer experience.

SLO MAP

Metric (technical)
  - Disk I/O Saturation  -> SLO: Response Time / Availability
  - Latency Spikes       -> SLO: Response Time
  - Error-rate Elevation-> SLO: Availability
  - Identity/Network Anomalies -> SLO: Security Posture

Standards focus: NIST-aligned monitoring/auditability practices and CIS benchmark-driven hardening support consistent telemetry collection and retention.

6. Actionability & Routing

Alert routing uses severity and SLO impact classes to ensure notifications reflect customer impact and operational priority.

ALERT ROUTING POLICY

If alert.severity == 'critical' and alert.slo_impact != 'low'
  then notify(oncall)
else
  retain for trend analysis + incident review
